rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Respondent's Motion for Further Response to Request for Document Production and for Sanctions is denied. There is insufficient showing that the amount of attorney's fees Petitioner has accrued over the previous twelve years is relevant to determining whether the amount of attorney's fees Respondent is seeking to have borne by the Trust is reasonable and necessary (Donahue v. Donahue (2010) 182 Cal. App. 4th 259). Further, per case law, the contents of the requested invoices is entirely covered by the attorney-client communications privilege (Los Angeles Cty. Bd. of Supervisors v. Sup. Ct (2016) 2 Cal. 5th 282). The competing sanctions requests are both denied. Respondent did not prevail on this motion, and is therefore not entitled to sanctions. Because Petitioner failed to comply with CCP section 2031.240, the establishment of the privilege claim was left to this motion. Accordingly, Petitioner's request for sanctions is also denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 16, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VISHWAS NARURKAR TRUST DATED JAN-24-2019
rulings.Rulings: Off calendar, in light of the Second Amended Petition having been filed August 28, 2020, on leave of court.
